[Though Kadamba and Nipa are considered as synonyms, here

An outlet for the water should be made on one side with the
sluice being built of stones; and a panel without apertures, flxed in
a frame and fastened to the earth with mud.
[The metre is
In the next two verses he gives a prescription for purifying
A mixture of antimony, and the powder of Bhadramusta (a
kind of grass) bulbs, andropogon, Rajako^ataka and myrobalan
combined with Kataka nuts should be dropped into a well (for
purifying the water).

526 Brhat Saihhiti

I Even the latter word has three meanings: antimony, sour
gruel and jujube. The mixture comprises six ingredients.]
